Listings appear and disappear quickly, and good deals are easy to miss.\n\n## Core Features\n\n### Watchlist Management\n- Add specific product searches (e.g., \"B&W HTM1 D3 Rosenut, used\")\n- Add broad category watches (e.g., \"B&W in-ceiling speakers\")\n- Set target price ranges and condition preferences\n- Enable/disable individual watches\n\n### Multi-Site Search\n- OfferUp\n- Facebook Marketplace\n- Craigslist\n- Audiogon\n- eBay\n- Extensible adapter pattern for adding new sites\n\n### Deal Discovery\n- Scheduled scraping runs (configurable, default daily)\n- Deduplication of listings across sites\n- Price tracking over time for repeated listings\n\n### Review Dashboard\n- Web UI to browse discovered listings\n- Filter by site, price, condition, watch category\n- Mark listings as \"interested\", \"dismissed\", or \"purchased\"\n- Direct links to original listings\n- Summary of new deals since last review\n\n## Non-Goals (Initial Release)\n\n- Mobile app\n- Push notifications / email / SMS\n- Price prediction or market analytics\n- Automated purchasing or bidding\n- User accounts / multi-user support\n"}"}],"details":{"id":"c16b61f7-cf7c-45f6-8626-7964ada87f47","name":"speakerSeeker","path":"/Users/tdmtrader/speakerSeeker","totalTracks":9,"completedTracks":1,"inProgressTracks":0,"totalTasks":72,"completedTasks":72,"progressPercentage":100,"goals":"# speakerSeeker

## Vision

A marketplace deal aggregator that monitors multiple online listing platforms for audio equipment, matches products against user-defined watchlists, and surfaces the best deals through a local web dashboard.

## Target Users

- Audiophiles and home theater enthusiasts searching for specific speaker models at good prices
- Buyers who want to monitor multiple marketplaces without manually checking each one daily

## Problem

Finding deals on specific audio equipment (especially used/discontinued models) requires manually browsing OfferUp, Facebook Marketplace, Craigslist, Audiogon, eBay, and other sites repeatedly. Listings appear and disappear quickly, and good deals are easy to miss.

## Core Features

### Watchlist Management
- Add specific product searches (e.g., "B&W HTM1 D3 Rosenut, used")
- Add broad category watches (e.g., "B&W in-ceiling speakers")
- Set target price ranges and condition preferences
- Enable/disable individual watches

### Multi-Site Search
- OfferUp
- Facebook Marketplace
- Craigslist
- Audiogon
- eBay
- Extensible adapter pattern for adding new sites

### Deal Discovery
- Scheduled scraping runs (configurable, default daily)
- Deduplication of listings across sites
- Price tracking over time for repeated listings

### Review Dashboard
- Web UI to browse discovered listings
- Filter by site, price, condition, watch category
- Mark listings as "interested", "dismissed", or "purchased"
- Direct links to original listings
- Summary of new deals since last review

## Non-Goals (Initial Release)

- Mobile app
- Push notifications / email / SMS
- Price prediction or market analytics
- Automated purchasing or bidding
- User accounts / multi-user support
"}}

Provide a reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure that can be rebuilt from this repo\n\n## Current State\n\n- **Server**: Physical Ubuntu Linux machine (`theborg.home`), SSH via `tdmtrader@theborg.home`\n- **Running services**: InfluxDB and Grafana (installed natively, not containerized)\n- **No Kubernetes** currently deployed\n\n## Key Features\n\n- K3s cluster bootstrap and configuration\n- Kubernetes manifests for all managed services\n- GitOps continuous deployment via ArgoCD\n- Concourse CI/CD pipeline definitions\n- Monitoring stack (InfluxDB + Grafana) running on k8s\n- Simple shell scripts for initial server/k3s setup (or manual setup)\n"}"}],"details":{"id":"911313c8-c2b5-425e-97cc-2d6844a8cd8b","name":"home-infra","path":"/Users/tdmtrader/home-infra","totalTracks":6,"completedTracks":2,"inProgressTracks":0,"totalTasks":48,"completedTasks":45,"progressPercentage":94,"goals":"# Product: home-infra

## Overview

Infrastructure-as-code repository for managing a single-node home server (physical Ubuntu machine) running a k3s Kubernetes cluster. The goal is to consolidate all services onto Kubernetes with GitOps-driven deployment and a CI/CD pipeline.

## Target Users

- **Primary**: Single maintainer (tdmtrader) managing personal home infrastructure

## Goals

1. Deploy and manage a single-node k3s cluster on a home Linux VM (`theborg.home`)
2. Migrate existing native services (InfluxDB, Grafana) to run on Kubernetes
3. Manage cluster workloads via GitOps (ArgoCD or similar)
4. Run Concourse CI/CD for building and deploying hosted projects
5. Provide a reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure that can be rebuilt from this repo

## Current State

- **Server**: Physical Ubuntu Linux machine (`theborg.home`), SSH via `tdmtrader@theborg.home`
- **Running services**: InfluxDB and Grafana (installed natively, not containerized)
- **No Kubernetes** currently deployed

## Key Features

- K3s cluster bootstrap and configuration
- Kubernetes manifests for all managed services
- GitOps continuous deployment via ArgoCD
- Concourse CI/CD pipeline definitions
- Monitoring stack (InfluxDB + Grafana) running on k8s
- Simple shell scripts for initial server/k3s setup (or manual setup)
"}}

**Track ID:** `deploy_speakerseeker_to_k3s_20260303`
**Type:** feature

## Overview

Deploy the speakerSeeker web application (a marketplace deal aggregator for audio equipment) to the home-infra k3s cluster. This involves creating a Concourse CI pipeline to build the container image, writing Kubernetes manifests, and configuring ArgoCD for GitOps-managed deployment.

All artifacts are written to the **home-infra** repository (`https://github.com/tdmtrader/home-infra.git`). The speakerSeeker source lives at `~/speakerSeeker` with an existing Dockerfile.

## Architecture

```
speakerSeeker repo (GitHub)
    │
    ▼ (trigger on push)
Concourse CI pipeline
    │ build image via oci-build-task
    ▼
registry.home (local Docker registry)
    │
    ▼ (image pull)
k3s cluster
    ├── Namespace: speakerseeker
    ├── Secret: speakerseeker-secrets (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
    ├── PVC: speakerseeker-data (SQLite DB)
    ├── Deployment: speakerseeker (1 replica, Recreate strategy)
    ├── Service: speakerseeker (ClusterIP, port 3000)
    ├── Ingress: speakerseeker.home (Traefik)
    └── CronJob: speakerseeker-scrape (periodic scraping, optional)
        │
        ▼
ArgoCD (app-of-apps)
    └── apps/speakerseeker.yaml → manifests/speakerseeker/
```

## Requirements

1. **CI Pipeline** — Concourse pipeline (`pipelines/speakerseeker.yaml`) that watches the speakerSeeker GitHub repo, builds the Docker image using `oci-build-task`, and pushes to `registry.home`
2. **K8s Secret** — Store `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as a Kubernetes Secret in the `speakerseeker` namespace
3. **Persistent Storage** — PVC mounted at `/data` for the SQLite database (`speakerseeker.db` + WAL files)
4. **Deployment** — Single-replica Deployment using `Recreate` strategy (SQLite doesn't support concurrent writes). Image: `registry.home/speakerseeker:latest`
5. **Service** — ClusterIP Service on port 3000
6. **Ingress** — Traefik Ingress at `speakerseeker.home`
7. **ArgoCD App** — Raw-manifest Application pointing to `manifests/speakerseeker/`, following the Plex pattern
8. **CronJob (optional)** — Periodic `POST /api/scrape` call for automated deal scraping

## Key Technical Details

### Container Image
- Base: `node:20-slim`, multi-stage build (deps → build → runner)
- Native addon: `better-sqlite3` requires `python3 make g++` at build time
- Runtime deps: `curl` (OfferUp scraper), Playwright Chromium (browser scraping)
- Exposes port 3000
- Image will be large (~800MB+) due to Playwright/Chromium

### Environment Variables
| Variable | Source | Required |
|----------|--------|----------|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | K8s Secret | Yes (for AI classification) |
| `DATABASE_PATH` | Hardcoded in Dockerfile as `/data/speakerseeker.db` | No |
| `NODE_ENV` | Set to `production` in Dockerfile | No |
| `PORT` | Set to `3000` in Dockerfile | No |
| `HOSTNAME` | Set to `0.0.0.0` in Dockerfile | No |

### Storage
- SQLite in WAL mode — must persist `.db`, `.db-shm`, `.db-wal` files
- PVC size: 1Gi (sufficient for listing data + thumbnails metadata)
- Deployment strategy must be `Recreate` to avoid concurrent SQLite access

### Networking
- Internal DNS: `speakerseeker.home`
- No TLS (internal network, matches existing pattern)
- No NodePort needed — ClusterIP + Ingress is sufficient

### Registry
- Push via in-cluster DNS: `registry-docker-registry.cicd.svc:5000/speakerseeker`
- Pull via k3s mirror: `registry.home/speakerseeker`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Concourse pipeline builds speakerSeeker image and pushes to `registry.home`
- [ ] `kubectl get pods -n speakerseeker` shows a running pod
- [ ] `curl http://speakerseeker.home` returns the Next.js dashboard
- [ ] SQLite data persists across pod restarts (PVC mounted at `/data`)
- [ ] ArgoCD shows the speakerseeker app as Healthy/Synced
- [ ] AI classification works (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret is mounted)

## Out of Scope

- TLS/HTTPS termination (no existing services use it internally)
- Horizontal scaling (SQLite is single-writer)
- External DNS or public internet exposure
- Database migration to PostgreSQL
- Helm chart — raw manifests are simpler and match the Plex pattern
